programmes.show_description: Alf returns home from the funeral of Else, his long-suffering wife. Having lost her disability allowance and half the pension, life ahead is not too rosy. S2 E1)
With Elvis Costello and David Essex. The legendary BAFTA-winning music and comedy show from Thames TV (1979). All in the best possible taste!

While in South America, Simon discovers a pair of con artists who stole a friend's savings and teaches them a valuable lesson while getting involved with local revolutionaries. S3 E19: The Golden Frog

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
Exploring claims of miraculous religious statues. Moving, weeping, healing, is this faith, illusion, or a miracle? Narrated by Robert Powell.
The heat is on during a hot bank holiday as an annual steam gala weekend approaches and keeping the 140-tonne engines moving safely proves a challenge. S3 E3
The F-4 Phantom II is an American tandem two-seat, twin-engine, long-range supersonic jet interceptor and fighter-bomber originally developed by McDonnell Aircraft for the United States Navy. S3 E5
Revealing life for guests and staff in a typical three-star British holiday hotel. S1 E1
The theories, conspiracies and facts relating to Rudolf Hess's dramatic flight to Scotland in 1941 and his subsequent death in Spandau prison. Ep6: Rudolf Hess
We look back to the bombing of London and other cities by the Luftwaffe in 1940 and 1941 by examining life between air raids and the effects they had on British society. Narrated by Michael Aspel. E3
The aftermath of every battle was human carnage. We hear the heartbreaking and demanding stories of the doctors and medical orderlies who tended to the wounded and dying. S1 E3: Saving The Wounded
In 1954, the USA exploded a hydrogen bomb in the South Pacific - a few years later, the USSR detonated a device even larger. We examine why these superpowers raced to build the world's biggest bomb.
The Battle of the Somme began in July 1916; more than three million men fought in the battle and one million men were wounded or killed, making it one of the deadliest battles in human history.
Codenamed Operation Overlord, the Battle of Normandy in 1944 - the invasion of Nazi-occupied Western Europe by the Allies - is one of the best-known battles of World War II and also known as D-Day.
We explore how the change of attitude on the political front in the USA and incidents involving Americans with combatants in the on-going fight led the United States to join this global conflict. E9
As Adolf Hitler clawed his way closer to power, his extremist policies brought him a host of enemies. S1 E2: Early Attempts on Hitler's Life
We reach the top 10 in our countdown of America's greatest unsolved mysteries including the assassination of Martin Luther King, a witch hunt in Salem, and the missing minutes on Nixon's tapes. E9
Jack helps Tom take on two ruffians who threaten him over money, and Jessie tries to persuade a widow to let her son take up a scholarship. S1 E12: Heads You Win, Tails I Lose
Arthur starts a courier service, the Daley Post, and it isn't long before its competitors start throwing the wrong sort of business at him. S9 E3: Uneasy Rider
Francis wrongly blames Ross for helping Verity elope with Andrew Blamey, and Mark suspects Dwight and Keren of having an affair and starts a chain of events. Starring Aidan Turner. S1 E7
Sam uncovers corruption in CID when he takes on a local crime boss. When the crime boss takes steps to stop Sam's investigation, will Gene do what is right? Starring John Simm. S1 E4
The long-suffering Johnsons help Alf out when he's shut out after coming home late from the pub. S2 E2
With Cliff Richard and Dave Edmunds. The legendary BAFTA-winning music and comedy show from Thames TV (1979). All in the best possible taste!
When an innkeeper's daughter asks for Simon's help, he uncovers ex-military personnel and the daughter's father that are all involved in an elaborate prison break. S3 E20: The Frightened Innkeeper

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
An unsinkable luxury sails toward tragedy as ambition and fate collide in disaster at sea. Narrated by Robert Powell.
It's August Bank Holiday on the railway; with the sun predicted, it's set to be a busy and lucrative few days. S3 E4
The Grumman TBF Avenger is an American WWII torpedo bomber developed initially for the United States Navy and Marine Corps and used by several air and naval aviation services around the world. S3 E6
Checking in to the Damson Dene are Tom and Susanna, who arrive a couple of weeks before their wedding to make sure everything is in place. S1 E2
The story of one of the most thrilling chapters in the history of World War II as a nation under the inspired leadership of Winston Churchill defies Adolf Hitler and a planned Nazi invasion.
The suffering endured by the people of London during the Blitz creates a new comradeship, Rommel triumphs in North Africa, and food rationing is introduced in Britain. Narrated by Michael Aspel. E4

The story of 1917's terrible battles at Arras and Passchendaele. Countless numbers drowned in mud during battles that remains synonymous with the horror of mechanised warfare. S1 E4: Horror In The Mud
Exploring the history of military tactics - water covers almost two-thirds of the Earth's surface, and assaults from the sea range from massive invasions to spectacular raids. S1 E1: Blitzkrieg
In 1916, Bapaume was one of the cities considered to be strategic objectives by the Allies in the framework of the Battle of the Somme; its capture was a turning point of the First World War.
...Were You? Princess Diana lived a fabled life and bore testimony to the commonality of the everyday person - Diana's story and the tragedy surrounding her death were key events of the 1990s.
We examine the end of the fighting and repercussions faced by each of the countries involved in the war, those issues that arose immediately after the war and the groundwork laid for the future. E10
By January 1933, Hitler's brutal rise to power had earned him many enemies, and the savage in-fighting between Nazi chieftains and his bodyguard were bloodthirsty. S1 E3: Kill the New Chancellor
We conclude the countdown of the greatest unsolved American mysteries and crimes with three cigars that changed the course of the American Civil War and a presidential assassination conspiracy. E10
Jack is released from prison; Matt arranges for a welcoming procession and Jack is paraded through the streets as 'The People's Friend' with hammer in hand. : Kind-Hearted Rat with a Lifebelt
Arthur gets arrested when he attempts to hunt down a new car, and acts very strangely in the process.
George expands the Warleggan empire as Francis and Elizabeth fall sick with a life-threatening illness and Demelza risks her own life to save them. Starring Aidan Turner. S1 E8

Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. The murder of a football fan results in a clash of methods as Sam and Gene go undercover in a local pub. Starring John Simm. S1 E5
